raw_text:
Ticket: SCRUM-64
Summary: Create automated data pipeline for Verizon analytics platform
Status: To Do
Priority: High
Assignee: Unassigned
Description:
Project Name: Verizon analytics platform Requirement Type: New_Development Stakeholder: John Objective Eliminate performance degradation from heavy analytical queries on production database and provide centralized analytics store with single source of truth Build a comprehensive data pipeline to extract data from verizon-data:us-central1:mysql-druid-metadatastore (Agentichub.employees) to BigQuery (verizon_data_dea.employees). The pipeline must perform a full historical load followed by daily incremental syncs using updated_at watermark column with UPSERT logic. All reads must be directed to read-replica to avoid production database performance impact. Expected Outcome Clean, deduplicated data available in BigQuery curated layer by 06:00 AM UTC daily with zero production database impact Connections and DB Details Source: verizon-data:us-central1:mysql-druid-metadatastore (IP: 34.70.79.163:3306), Database: Agentichub, Table: employees. Target: BigQuery project verizon-data, dataset verizon_data_dea, table employees Source Table: EMPLOYEES Connection Details: verizon-data:us-central1:mysql-druid-metadatastore, 34.70.79.163, 3306 Source Username: sa Acceptance Criteria Success Conditions: Row count in BigQuery equals source table count with 0% tolerance Airflow DAG completes before 06:00 AM UTC daily dbt uniqueness test passes with 0 failures on primary key Connection string points only to replica host Validation Rules: COUNT(*) reconciliation between source and target with zero tolerance Primary key (id) must be not null and unique in curated layer Foreign key referential integrity checks for order_items.order_id and payments.order_id

Objective: Eliminate performance degradation from heavy analytical queries on production database and provide centralized analytics store with single source of truth by building a comprehensive data pipeline to extract data from MySQL to BigQuery with full historical load followed by daily incremental syncs

Business Context:
Heavy analytical queries are degrading production database performance. Organization needs a centralized analytics store with single source of truth to separate analytical workloads from production systems while maintaining data consistency and availability.

Functional Requirements:
Build data pipeline from MySQL to BigQuery
Perform full historical load of employee data
Implement daily incremental syncs using updated_at watermark column
Use UPSERT logic for data synchronization
Direct all reads to read-replica to avoid production impact

Implement row count reconciliation with zero tolerance
Validate primary key uniqueness and not null constraints
Perform foreign key referential integrity checks
